title: agropyron caninum var. andinum
intro: Agropyron caninum var. andinum, also known as the Andean wheatgrass, is a subspecies of the Agropyron caninum plant that is native to the Andean region in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory conditions, due to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The Andean wheatgrass can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as an ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.
